criteria.description: to_roman(n) returns correct Roman numeral for 1..3999 with subtractive notation
criteria.evidence: roman.py lines 71-82: pairs list includes all subtractive forms (900→CM, 400→CD, 90→XC, 40→XL, 9→IX, 4→IV) in strict descending order. Greedy algorithm correctly iterates and builds result. Test output confirms: 12 conversions passed including 4→IV, 1987→MCMLXXXVII, 3999→MMMCMXCIX examples.

criteria.description: to_roman(n) raises ValueError for n < 1 or n > 3999
criteria.evidence: roman.py lines 68-69: range guard raises ValueError before conversion logic. Test output: 3 range checks passed.
test_output.txt line 1-3: TESTS: PASSED, [PASS] python3 test_roman.py, exit message shows OK: 12 conversions + 3 range checks passed.

tests.summary: python3 test_roman.py passed: 12 conversions + 3 range checks
